title: eschscholzia crocea var. rosea
intro: Eschscholzia crocea var. rosea, also known as the California poppy, is a rare and delicate variety of the California poppy plant, which is native to the coastal regions of California and Oregon. This plant is known for its vibrant orange or pink flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The medicinal properties of Eschscholzia crocea var. rosea include its ability to soothe and calm the nervous system, relieving anxiety and insomnia, and its potential to reduce pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and support overall well-being.
